theorems.name: A1
theorems.statement: If a factor expands with fixed K, complexity C is non-decreasing

theorems.name: A2
theorems.statement: If constraints tighten, complexity cannot increase

theorems.name: A3
theorems.statement: Under factorised constraints and binning, total complexity is multiplicative

theorems.name: A4
theorems.statement: Discrete factor introductions produce jumps with Delta log2 C = log2(k)

theorems.name: B1
theorems.statement: Selection pressure that reduces rho^op makes I_g^op non-decreasing

theorems.name: B2
theorems.statement: Discrete change gives Delta I_g^op = log2(rho_minus / rho_plus)

theorems.name: B3
theorems.statement: Discrete pressure events produce jump discontinuities in I_g^op

theorems.name: C1
theorems.statement: Change in I_g^op decomposes into log2(C ratio) minus log2(A ratio)

theorems.name: C2
theorems.statement: Joint intervention reduction equals sum of single-term reductions

assumptions: Finite decision resolution induces finite configuration spaces
Distinct options are equivalence classes induced by the binning map b
Constraint tightening means K2 implies K1 at configuration level
A3 exactness assumes factorised constraints and factorised binning across subsystems
All log arguments are strictly positive
Selection pressure is modelled by antitone rho^op
Event times are discrete and updates occur at event indices
Joint intervention uses alpha in (0,1) and beta greater than 1
